gene_description: Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor — neurotrophin critical for energy homeostasis in the hypothalamus and cognitive control of eating behaviors. Variants reduce activity-induced BDNF expression, affecting appetite regulation and weight.

source.title: rs10767664 Gene Variant in Brain-Derived Neurotrophic Factor Is Associated with Diabetes Mellitus Type 2 in Caucasian Females with Obesity
source.finding: BDNF rs10767664 T allele carriers show higher BMI, increased emotional eating, and 1.33x diabetes risk adjusted for age and BMI. The variant impairs activity-induced BDNF expression and alters noradrenaline transporter availability in hypothalamus.

TT.effect: Homozygous T carriers have significantly reduced activity-induced BDNF expression. Associated with higher BMI, increased emotional eating scores, higher risk of type 2 diabetes (OR 1.33), and altered noradrenaline transporter availability in hypothalamus and locus coeruleus. After hypocaloric diet, show different metabolic response patterns.
TT.recommendation: Behavioral eating interventions focused on emotional eating patterns. Mediterranean-style hypocaloric diet shows metabolic benefits. GLP-1 agonists may provide additional appetite control. Mindful eating practices. Regular physical activity (exercise itself boosts BDNF).

AT.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate BDNF expression. Mildly increased emotional eating tendency and BMI. Modest increase in metabolic risk.
AT.recommendation: Standard healthy diet with attention to emotional eating triggers. Regular exercise to boost BDNF naturally. Monitor weight and metabolic markers.

TA.effect: Heterozygous carriers show intermediate BDNF expression. Mildly increased emotional eating tendency and BMI. Modest increase in metabolic risk.
TA.recommendation: Standard healthy diet with attention to emotional eating triggers. Regular exercise to boost BDNF naturally. Monitor weight and metabolic markers.

AA.effect: Wild-type with standard BDNF expression and activity-induced regulation. Normal appetite control and energy homeostasis from this locus.
